payload.text: by inspecting the workload and search space in parallel.
have a clear picture. ViT block is transformer

iple levers targeting different angles:

1. **Max aut

ologue fusion** — ViT is GEMM-heavy.
2. **FX rewrites
ordinate descent tuning + GELU tanh appro

payload.result: plan #0 is missing a non-empty `interventions` list

payload.text: batch tool expects `interventions` as a key, not `inter
ventions_json`. Let me try the right shape:

payload.description: Max-autotune GEMMs with epilogue/prologue fusion + shape padding (ViT is GEMM-bound)
payload.expected_effect: Better GEMM kernels for QKV/proj/MLP matmuls; epilogue fusion folds bias+add into matmul

payload.description: FX rewrites: tanh-approx GELU + LN fused with residual + comprehensive padding
payload.expected_effect: Cheaper GELU and one-fewer kernel by fusing LN with prior residual add

payload.description: Aggressive fusion + coordinate-descent tuning + tanh GELU
payload.expected_effect: More fused kernels & per-kernel block/warp tuning around the small (32*197) sequence

rows.rationale: Better GEMM kernels for QKV/proj/MLP matmuls; epilogue fusion folds bias+add into matmul
Cheaper GELU and one-fewer kernel by fusing LN with prior residual add

payload.text:
| Candidate | Median ms | Speedup |
|---|-----------|-----------|---------|
| 1 | Max-autotune GEMMs + epilogue/

prologue fusion + shape padding | 0.1581 | 1.031× |
| 2 | FX rewrites: tanh G

Aggressive fusion + coord-descent tuning + tanh GELU + loop-ordering** | **0.1577** | **1.033×** |

**

ever candidates succeeded with correctness preserved (`max_abs_diff = 0.0`).
- Best

so far is **cand-b806dde080** at **1.033× speedup**.
- Per-target-
ge out plain `knob` interventions (avg 1.0317×) — the GELU tanh approximation and

LN-residual fusion are pulling weight.
- No failure targets —

search space is well-behaved.
- Speedups are tight
ly clustered (~3%), suggesting the baseline is already close to G
EMM-bound on this small encoder block; remaining head
room likely lies in attention-specific rewrites (e.g., S
DPA fusion) or per-kernel block/warp tuning of the LayerNorm + trans

pose kernels rather than further config knobs.

**Recommended
next step (1 slot remaining):** combine the winning ingredients — `
True` — to see if the gains stack.

workload.title: ViT-B/16 single encoder block
workload.description: One ViT-B/16 transformer encoder block (LayerNorm + MHA + LayerNorm + MLP) on an embedded sequence — bf16 on tensor cores. Faster compile and bench cycle than the full model.

tolerance.notes: bf16-vs-bf16 forward pass; baseline-vs-fp32-reference is recorded but non-gating.

leaderboard.rationale: More fused kernels & per-kernel block/warp tuning around the small (32*197) sequence
Cheaper GELU and one-fewer kernel by fusing LN with prior residual add
Better GEMM kernels for QKV/proj/MLP matmuls; epilogue fusion folds bias+add into matmul

device.name: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

args.plans_json: [{"interventions_json": "[{\"type\": \"knob\", \"selector\": \"inductor.aggressive_fusion\", \"value\": true}, {\"type\": \"choices\", \"selector\": \"triton_per_fused_add_native_layer_norm_transpose_view_5\", \"value\": {\"kernel\": \"triton_per_fused_add_native_layer_norm_transpose_view_5\", \"BLOCK_SIZE\": 128, \"num_warps\": 4, \"num_stages\": 3}}]", "description": "Enable aggressive fusion and tune the dominant kernel for better occupancy and memory access.", "expected_effect": "Reduced kernel launch overhead and improved memory locality."}, {"interventions_json": "[{\"type\": \"knob\", \"selector\": \"inductor.force_layout_optimization\", \"value\": true}, {\"type\": \"knob\", \"selector\": \"inductor.epilogue_fusion\", \"value\": true}, {\"type\": \"knob\", \"selector\": \"inductor.reorder_for_locality\", \"value\": true}]", "description": "Force layout optimization and enable epilogue fusion to improve memory access patterns.", "expected_effect": "Better memory layouts and reduced redundant memory operations."}, {"interventions_json": "[{\"type\": \"knob\", \"selector\": \"inductor.max_autotune_gemm\", \"value\": true}, {\"type\": \"knob\", \"selector\": \"inductor.max_autotune_pointwise\", \"value\": true}, {\"type\": \"knob\", \"selector\": \"inductor.max_autotune_gemm_backends\", \"value\": \"ATEN,TRITON,CPP\"}]", "description": "Enable GEMM and pointwise autotuning to optimize matrix multiplications and pointwise operations.", "expected_effect": "Faster matrix multiplications and pointwise operations."}]

args.plans_json: [{"interventions": [{"target_kind": "knob", "selector": "inductor.max_autotune_gemm_search_space", "value": "EXHAUSTIVE"}, {"target_kind": "knob", "selector": "inductor.max_autotune_gemm", "value": true}, {"target_kind": "knob", "selector": "inductor.max_autotune_gemm_backends", "value": "TRITON"}], "description": "Enable exhaustive GEMM autotuning with Triton-only backend to maximize tensor core usage."}, {"interventions": [{"target_kind": "knob", "selector": "inductor.prologue_fusion", "value": true}, {"target_kind": "knob", "selector": "inductor.epilogue_fusion", "value": false}, {"target_kind": "knob", "selector": "dynamo.automatic_dynamic_shapes", "value": true}], "description": "Enable prologue fusion and dynamic shape handling while disabling epilogue fusion to reduce overhead."}, {"interventions": [{"target_kind": "lowering", "selector": "aten.gelu", "value": {"replacement": "torch._inductor.lowering:gelu_triton"}}, {"target_kind": "lowering", "selector": "aten.layer_norm", "value": {"replacement": "torch._inductor.lowering:layer_norm_triton"}}], "description": "Replace GELU and LayerNorm lowering with custom Triton kernels for better performance."}]

payload.description: Enable exhaustive GEMM autotuning with Triton-only backend to maximize tensor core usage.
Enable prologue fusion and dynamic shape handling while disabling epilogue fusion to reduce overhead.
Replace GELU and LayerNorm lowering with custom Triton kernels for better performance.

rows.rationale: Enable exhaustive GEMM autotuning with Triton-only backend to maximize tensor core usage.
Enable GEMM and pointwise autotuning to optimize matrix multiplications and pointwise operations.

workload.title: ViT-B/16 single encoder block
workload.description: One ViT-B/16 transformer encoder block (LayerNorm + MHA + LayerNorm + MLP) on an embedded sequence — bf16 on tensor cores. Faster compile and bench cycle than the full model.

tolerance.notes: bf16-vs-bf16 forward pass; baseline-vs-fp32-reference is recorded but non-gating.

leaderboard.rationale: Enable exhaustive GEMM autotuning with Triton-only backend to maximize tensor core usage.
Enable GEMM and pointwise autotuning to optimize matrix multiplications and pointwise operations.
Enable aggressive fusion and tune the dominant kernel for better occupancy and memory access.
Replace GELU and LayerNorm lowering with custom Triton kernels for better performance.
Force layout optimization and enable epilogue fusion to improve memory access patterns.
Enable prologue fusion and dynamic shape handling while disabling epilogue fusion to reduce overhead.
